working_definition: An early fetal basal keratinocyte of the interfollicular epidermis, present from 7 PCW and decreasing after 11 PCW during the embryonic-to-fetal skin transition. Expresses basal keratins KRT5 and KRT14 alongside embryonic keratins KRT18, KRT17, and KRT19 not found in adult interfollicular epidermis, reflecting an immature, incompletely committed differentiation state.

best_match.cl_definition: A mitotically active, columnar-shaped keratinocyte located in the stratum basale of the epidermis and attached to the basement membrane.

justification: CL:0002187 describes the general basal cell of epidermis. The 'Immature basal' cluster represents the early fetal subset of this population, distinguished by co-expression of embryonic keratins (KRT18, KRT17, KRT19) and temporal restriction to early gestation (<11 PCW). The CL term does not distinguish fetal from adult basal cells, making this a broad match.

other_candidates.reason_rejected: Too narrow: the immature basal population includes transit-amplifying cells and is defined by its early developmental timing, not exclusively by stem cell quiescence.

recommendation: Use CL:0002187 (basal cell of epidermis) as a broad match. A more specific term capturing the early fetal basal keratinocyte state with embryonic keratin co-expression could be considered.
